answer:
You know, I really think that helping each other works better than just trying to win individually. I mean, the world is so interconnected, and by supporting one another, we create a more positive environment where everyone can thrive. For instance, during my last year in high school, we had this group project for our science class. Instead of competing against each other for the best grade, we decided to collaborate. It was incredible to see how much more we achieved when we combined our strengths. I remember one of my classmates was particularly good at research, while another excelled in presentations. By helping each other out, we not only got a higher grade but actually learned a lot more about the topic.

Also, I think that relying on collaboration over competition fosters better relationships. You build trust and respect with those around you, which is essential whether you're heading to university or entering the workforce. In today's society, teamwork is often valued more than individual achievements, especially in fields like medicine or technology, where collective efforts can lead to groundbreaking innovations.

But, to be fair, I get why some people might lean towards winning; it feels great to be recognized for your hard work. Still, the benefits of mutual support outweigh the fleeting satisfaction of a solo victory. Overall, I believe that when we help each other rather than just striving to win, we create not only a healthier community but also pave the way for long-term success together.
title: Which works better - trying to win or helping each other? Why do you think this?
